raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "Evoking a reflective, philosophical stance on temporality that reframes loss and possession as inapplicable to non-present time",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "Only the present can properly be said to be possessed or parted with",
    "Past and future lack the ontological status of objects that can be relinquished",
    "Human relation to time is limited such that 'parting' applies solely to the immediate moment"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "Internally consistent within a framework that distinguishes present from non-present time as categorically different in terms of ownership and loss",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "When deployed in contemplative or therapeutic contexts to encourage detachment from regret or anxiety over uncontrollable temporal domains",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "If read as a literal denial of memory, anticipation, or the practical consequences of past actions and future planning"
}
